name: bowtie
description: A Pulumi package for managing Bowtie ZTNA resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`bowtie` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/bowtieworks/terraform-provider-bowtie).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for managing Bowtie ZTNA resources.
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/bowtieworks/terraform-provider-bowtie)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-bowtie` repo](https://github.com/bowtieworks/pulumi-bowtie/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-bowtie` repo](https://github.com/bowtieworks/terraform-provider-bowtie/issues).

python.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/bowtieworks/terraform-provider-bowtie)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-bowtie` repo](https://github.com/bowtieworks/pulumi-bowtie/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-bowtie` repo](https://github.com/bowtieworks/terraform-provider-bowtie/issues).

caBundle.description:
A PEM-encoded CA bundle (inline contents or a path to a file) used to verify the Controller's TLS certificate, for
Controllers issued by a private certificate authority. Honors the `BOWTIE_CA_BUNDLE` environment variable if set.

host.description:
The Bowtie HTTP Controller endpoint. Honors the `BOWTIE_HOST` environment variable if set. Example:
`https://bowtie.example.com`

insecure.description:
Skip TLS certificate verification when connecting to the Controller. Honors the `BOWTIE_INSECURE` environment variable
if set. Intended for development controllers with self-signed certificates; do not enable against production.

taggedLocations.description: Control whether the provider will send policy resource locations using the new tagged type format or legacy format.

comment.description: An optional comment describing this member.

expires.description: An optional RFC 3339 timestamp after which the Controller automatically removes this member.

location.description: The location this member matches. Set exactly one of `ip`, `cidr`, `dns`, or `collection`.

name.description: A human readable name for this member.

collection.description: The ID of another collection to nest inside this one.

name.description: Name to exclude sending to the upstream server for resolution.

order.description: Order when presented with other excluded names in the web interface

addr.description: The IP address for this DNS server.

order.description: The order for this DNS server.

always.description: Match every device, authenticated or not.

ands.description: Match if all of the listed operands match. Each operand is a single matcher or a further and/or/nor group.

authenticatedUser.description: Match any device attached to an authenticated user.

device.description: Match a single device by ID.

deviceGroup.description: Match any device in the device group with this ID.

id.description: Internal identifier of the source predicate. Assigned by the provider.

nors.description: Match if none of the listed operands match. Each operand is a single matcher or a further and/or/nor group.

ors.description: Match if at least one of the listed operands match. Each operand is a single matcher or a further and/or/nor group.

user.description: Match a single user by ID.

userGroup.description: Match any user in the user group with this ID.

cidr.description: A CIDR address reachable from behind your Bowtie Controller.

collection.description: The ID of a collection whose members this resource should match. Requires the default tagged location format.

dns.description: A DNS name pointing to a resource reachable from behind your Bowtie Controller.

ip.description: The IP address of a resource reachable from behind your Bowtie Controller.

ranges.description: First element is the low port and second is the high port (range is inclusive).

provider.description:
The provider type for the bowtie package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

caBundle.description:
A PEM-encoded CA bundle (inline contents or a path to a file) used to verify the Controller's TLS certificate, for
Controllers issued by a private certificate authority. Honors the `BOWTIE_CA_BUNDLE` environment variable if set.

host.description:
The Bowtie HTTP Controller endpoint. Honors the `BOWTIE_HOST` environment variable if set. Example:
`https://bowtie.example.com`

insecure.description:
Skip TLS certificate verification when connecting to the Controller. Honors the `BOWTIE_INSECURE` environment variable
if set. Intended for development controllers with self-signed certificates; do not enable against production.

taggedLocations.description: Control whether the provider will send policy resource locations using the new tagged type format or legacy format.

caBundle.description:
A PEM-encoded CA bundle (inline contents or a path to a file) used to verify the Controller's TLS certificate, for
Controllers issued by a private certificate authority. Honors the `BOWTIE_CA_BUNDLE` environment variable if set.

host.description:
The Bowtie HTTP Controller endpoint. Honors the `BOWTIE_HOST` environment variable if set. Example:
`https://bowtie.example.com`

insecure.description:
Skip TLS certificate verification when connecting to the Controller. Honors the `BOWTIE_INSECURE` environment variable
if set. Intended for development controllers with self-signed certificates; do not enable against production.

taggedLocations.description: Control whether the provider will send policy resource locations using the new tagged type format or legacy format.

description.description: An optional description of the collection.

members.description: The locations contained in this collection.

name.description: The human readable name of the collection.

description.description: An optional description of the collection.

members.description: The locations contained in this collection.

name.description: The human readable name of the collection.

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering Collection resources.

description.description: An optional description of the collection.

members.description: The locations contained in this collection.

name.description: The human readable name of the collection.

allowTemporaryConsoleUsers.description: Whether temporary console users may be created on this Controller.

canUseIdp.description: Whether this Controller may use the identity provider.

canUsePublicHttps.description: Whether this Controller may serve public HTTPS.

canUseVanityDomain.description: Whether this Controller may use a vanity domain.

currentVersion.description: The software version the Controller is currently running.

persistentKeepalive.description: Keepalive interval, in seconds, advertised to peers.

publicAddress.description: The publicly reachable address clients and other Controllers use to reach this Controller.

siteId.description: The site this Controller belongs to.

sshListener.description: Where the Controller accepts SSH: `any`, `local-only`, or `tunnel-only`.

versionIncludePrereleases.description: Consider pre-release versions when selecting updates. Unset falls back to the organization default.

versionMinimumAge.description:
Minimum age, in days, a release must have before this Controller adopts it under a latest-version strategy. Unset falls
back to the organization default.

versionStrategySplayType.description: Spreads update times across a fleet. One of `no-delay`, `randomized-delay`, or `consistent-randomized-delay`.

versionStrategySplayValue.description: A systemd time-span used by `randomized-delay` and `consistent-randomized-delay`.

versionStrategyType.description:
How this Controller chooses update versions. One of `org-default`, `manual`, `specific`, `newest-at-interval`, or
`newest-at-calendar`. This is the upgrade orchestration control.

versionStrategyValue.description:
The value for `version_strategy_type` when it requires one: a version string for `specific`, a systemd time-span for
`newest-at-interval`, or a systemd calendar expression for `newest-at-calendar`. Leave unset for `org-default` and
`manual`.

webFilterTrustedProxyCollection.description: A collection ID used as the web-filter trusted proxy list for this Controller.

wireguardPort.description: The UDP port the Controller listens on for VPN connections.

wireguardStrategy.description: How the VPN port is reached: `static` (publicly reachable) or `dynamic` (privately reachable or NAT-punched).

allowTemporaryConsoleUsers.description: Whether temporary console users may be created on this Controller.

canUseIdp.description: Whether this Controller may use the identity provider.

canUsePublicHttps.description: Whether this Controller may serve public HTTPS.

canUseVanityDomain.description: Whether this Controller may use a vanity domain.

persistentKeepalive.description: Keepalive interval, in seconds, advertised to peers.

publicAddress.description: The publicly reachable address clients and other Controllers use to reach this Controller.

siteId.description: The site this Controller belongs to.

sshListener.description: Where the Controller accepts SSH: `any`, `local-only`, or `tunnel-only`.

versionIncludePrereleases.description: Consider pre-release versions when selecting updates. Unset falls back to the organization default.

versionMinimumAge.description:
Minimum age, in days, a release must have before this Controller adopts it under a latest-version strategy. Unset falls
back to the organization default.

versionStrategySplayType.description: Spreads update times across a fleet. One of `no-delay`, `randomized-delay`, or `consistent-randomized-delay`.

versionStrategySplayValue.description: A systemd time-span used by `randomized-delay` and `consistent-randomized-delay`.

versionStrategyType.description:
How this Controller chooses update versions. One of `org-default`, `manual`, `specific`, `newest-at-interval`, or
`newest-at-calendar`. This is the upgrade orchestration control.

versionStrategyValue.description:
The value for `version_strategy_type` when it requires one: a version string for `specific`, a systemd time-span for
`newest-at-interval`, or a systemd calendar expression for `newest-at-calendar`. Leave unset for `org-default` and
`manual`.

webFilterTrustedProxyCollection.description: A collection ID used as the web-filter trusted proxy list for this Controller.

wireguardPort.description: The UDP port the Controller listens on for VPN connections.

wireguardStrategy.description: How the VPN port is reached: `static` (publicly reachable) or `dynamic` (privately reachable or NAT-punched).

stateInputs.description: Input properties used for looking up and filtering Controller resources.

allowTemporaryConsoleUsers.description: Whether temporary console users may be created on this Controller.

canUseIdp.description: Whether this Controller may use the identity provider.

canUsePublicHttps.description: Whether this Controller may serve public HTTPS.

canUseVanityDomain.description: Whether this Controller may use a vanity domain.

publicAddress.description: The publicly reachable address clients and other Controllers use to reach this Controller.

siteId.description: The site this Controller belongs to.

versionIncludePrereleases.description: Consider pre-release versions when selecting updates. Unset falls back to the organization default.

versionMinimumAge.description:
Minimum age, in days, a release must have before this Controller adopts it under a latest-version strategy. Unset falls
back to the organization default.

versionStrategySplayType.description: Spreads update times across a fleet. One of `no-delay`, `randomized-delay`, or `consistent-randomized-delay`.

versionStrategySplayValue.description: A systemd time-span used by `randomized-delay` and `consistent-randomized-delay`.

versionStrategyType.description:
How this Controller chooses update versions. One of `org-default`, `manual`, `specific`, `newest-at-interval`, or
`newest-at-calendar`. This is the upgrade orchestration control.

versionStrategyValue.description:
The value for `version_strategy_type` when it requires one: a version string for `specific`, a systemd time-span for
`newest-at-interval`, or a systemd calendar expression for `newest-at-calendar`. Leave unset for `org-default` and
`manual`.

webFilterTrustedProxyCollection.description: A collection ID used as the web-filter trusted proxy list for this Controller.

wireguardPort.description: The UDP port the Controller listens on for VPN connections.

wireguardStrategy.description: How the VPN port is reached: `static` (publicly reachable) or `dynamic` (privately reachable or NAT-punched).

includeOnlySites.description: Limit name resolution for this domain only to these sites.

isCounted.description: Whether to only log metrics for this domain and not all requests.

isDns64.description: Whether to resolve names using DNS64.

isDropA.description: Whether to drop A record responses from requests for this domain.

isDropAll.description: Whether all record responses for this domain should be dropped.

isLog.description: Whether to log all requests for names in this domain.

isSearchDomain.description: Whether this domain should be treated as a search domain.

allowControllerApprovalWithPskOnly.description: Automatically admit a Controller that presents a valid pre-shared key, for zero-touch cluster scale-out.

allowDeviceApprovalOnUserAuth.description: Automatically approve a device when its user authenticates after installing the client.

controllerSshListener.description: Organization default for how Controllers listen on the SSH port: `any`, `local-only`, or `tunnel-only`.

controllerVersionIncludePrereleases.description: Default for considering pre-release versions when Controllers do not specify their own value.

controllerVersionMinimumAge.description: Default minimum age, in days, a release must have before adoption under a latest-version strategy.

controllerVersionStrategyType.description:
Organization default update strategy: `manual`, `specific`, `newest-at-interval`, or `newest-at-calendar`.
(`org-default` is not valid here; the organization default cannot itself defer to the organization default.)

controllerVersionStrategyValue.description: Value for the default update strategy when it requires one (a version, time-span, or calendar expression).

deleteDevicesMoreStaleThanDays.description: Delete devices that have not checked in for more than this many days. Must be greater than 7.

disablePeersRequireQuorum.description: When false, destructive device cleanup only proceeds when a quorum of Controller peers is connected.

peersRequireQuorumPercentage.description: Percentage (0-100) of Controller peers required for quorum. Default 51.

removeApprovalDevicesMoreStaleThanDays.description: Revoke approval for devices that have not checked in for more than this many days. Must be greater than 7.

userDeviceDisassociationMinutes.description: Disassociate a device from its user after this many minutes.

action.description: The action to take on matching traffic: `Accept`, `Reject` (deny with feedback), or `Drop` (deny silently).

dest.description: The ID of the resource group this policy controls access to.

order.description: Evaluation order of the policy. When omitted, the Controller appends the policy to the end of the list.

source.description:
The set of devices this policy applies to. Set exactly one of the leaf matchers (`always`, `authenticated_user`, `user`,
`device`, `user_group`, `device_group`) or exactly one of the boolean groups (`and`, `or`, `nor`).

status.description: Whether the policy is `Enabled` or `Disabled`. Defaults to `Enabled`.

applyStrategy.description:
Rollout strategy: `always`, `never` (staged but inactive), `percentage_user_match`, or `percentage_device_match`.
Defaults to `always`.

applyStrategyPercentage.description:
The match percentage (0-255, in ~0.5% increments) used when `apply_strategy` is `percentage_user_match` or
`percentage_device_match`.

collectionId.description: The ID of the collection whose CIDR members are excluded from the tunnel. The collection must already exist.

matchOnlyDeviceGroups.description: Apply only to devices in these device group IDs.

matchOnlyDeviceOs.description:
Apply only to devices with this OS: `windows`, `macos`, `linux`, `ios`, `android`, `chromeos`, `bowtie.Controller`, or
`unknown`. Changing or removing this recreates the exclusion.

matchOnlyDeviceType.description:
Apply only to devices of this type: `laptop`, `desktop`, `mobile`, `server`, or `other`. Changing or removing this
recreates the exclusion.

matchOnlyOwnership.description: Apply only to devices owned by this organization ID. Changing or removing this recreates the exclusion.

matchOnlyUserGroups.description: Apply only to devices whose user is in these user group IDs.

name.description: Human-readable name for the exclusion.

onlyIfWanMatchesCidrs.description:
If set, the exclusion only applies when the device's WAN IP falls within one of these CIDRs (for example, an office
network).

sites.description: The site IDs this exclusion applies to. Omit to apply to all sites.

version.description: A content hash of the exclusion, computed by the Controller.

role.description: What role the user is assigned. Value must be one of `Ownder`, `User`, `FullAdministrator`, or `LimitedAdministrator`.
